results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THE PROPOSED RESEARCH IS TO ADDRESS PER- AND POLYFLUOROALKYL SUBSTANCES (PFAS) EXPOSURE VIA INDOOR ENVIRONMENTS BY EVALUATING PFAS IN MULTIPLE RESIDENTIAL MEDIA AND ESTIMATING ITS CONTRIBUTION TO TOTAL PFAS EXPOSURE. ACTIVITIES:THE PROJECT WILL USE DIETARY AND QUESTIONNAIRE DATA TO ESTIMATE EXPOSURES FROM PERSONAL CARE PRODUCTS, FOODS AND FOOD PACKAGING. BY MEASURING CONCENTRATIONS OF PFAS IN ENVIRONMENTAL SAMPLES USING A FULLY VALIDATED, MULTI-FACETED LC-MS/MS METHOD WITH ISOTOPE DILUTION CALIBRATION THE PROJECT WILL BE ABLE TO ESTIMATE THE OVERALL EXPOSURES AND RECONCILE THE EXPOSURE WITH BIOLOGICAL MEASUREMENTS. SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:THE ANTICIPATED DELIVERABLES ARE ANNUAL PROGRESS AND FINAL REPORTS. PROJECT OUTPUTS INCLUDE A VALIDATED, STANDARDIZED PROTOCOL AND SOP FOR MEASURING A TARGETED SET OF PFAS IN THE RESIDENTIAL ENVIRONMENT AND EXPOSURE MEASUREMENT DATA CONCORDANT WITH BIOMONITORING DATA FOR A TARGETED SET OF PFAS CHEMICALS IN AN AFRICAN AMERICAN PREGNANT STUDY POPULATION. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES INCLUDE STATE AND LOCAL PUBLIC HEALTH MANAGERS, THE SCIENTIFIC COMMUNITY, AND COMMUNITIES AFFECTED BY RESIDENTIAL PFAS EXPOSURE.
